hazard.summary: Genkit retries RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED / HTTP 429 responses with fixed exponential timing and does not honor provider Retry-After headers, causing repeated requests inside the server-imposed cooldown window.
signals: Retry delay computation does not consult the provider's Retry-After header.
A provider can return HTTP 429 with Retry-After: 60.
Observed behavior: the middleware retries at roughly 1000ms intervals during that 60-second cooldown.
OpenAI and Gemini also send Retry-After.

constraints: When a provider supplies Retry-After on a 429 / RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED response, the client should not retry before that cooldown expires.
The issue applies across providers that emit Retry-After, not just a single backend.
Pure exponential backoff is insufficient when explicit server pacing information is available.
knobs: Retry status set includes RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED by default.
Retry delay/backoff computation is the relevant control point.
Honor Retry-After header when present; otherwise fall back to normal exponential backoff.
verification: Trigger a 429 response that includes Retry-After: 60 and confirm the next retry is scheduled no earlier than 60 seconds later.
Verify the client does not emit repeated retry attempts at ~1 second intervals during the Retry-After window.
Repeat against providers known to send Retry-After headers, including Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini.
Confirm fallback behavior still uses exponential backoff when Retry-After is absent.
